headline: EIA STEO April 2026: Hormuz disruption reduces global oil supply by 3%
body: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East has severely disrupted oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz. Flows fell from approximately 20 million b/d pre-war to an average of 2.7 million b/d in March 2026. Brent crude reached an all-time high of $144/barrel in early April. The EIA has raised its full-year 2026 Brent forecast to $96/bbl, up from $78.84 in March, with Q2 2026 averaging $114.60/bbl. Shut-in production averaged 9.1 million b/d in March 2026.
